title: ESPuino
restartinfo:
ESPuino is being restarted...
Page reloads automatically after restart finished.
shutdowninfo:
ESPuino is now switched off...
Page reloads automatically when the ESPuino is switched on again.

toast.conlost: Connection to ESPuino broken! Please reload website.

ir.title: Infrared remote control
ir.intro: Teach the buttons of your remote and assign each one a command. Click “Learn”, then press the button on the remote — the received code is filled in automatically. Don't forget to save.
ir.longPressHint: How long a button must be held to trigger the long-press action (200–5000 ms). Buttons without a long action still react instantly.
ir.learnPrompt: Press a button on the remote now…
ir.empty: No buttons learned yet. Click “Learn button” to add one.
ir.timeout: No IR signal received — learning cancelled.
ir.duplicate: The same code is mapped more than once; the first match wins.

led.title: LED settings
led.numidledots: Number of LEDs in idle state

oled.title: OLED display
oled.enablehint: When disabled the panel is powered off (e.g. for night-time).

rfid.modDesc: Pick a modification action, then lay the card. Music cards are learned directly in the file browser (right-click → Learn RFID).

password.title: Password
password.keep: Leave empty to keep the current password
password.desc: One shared password for everything: web interface, FTP and WebDAV. For FTP and WebDAV the username does not matter - any username works, only this password is checked. Once logged in to the web interface you stay logged in for 90 days; protection is inactive in hotspot mode. The password is never shown again; leave the field empty to keep it. To turn protection off everywhere, tick “Disable password protection” and save.
password.nochange: Nothing to save: enter a new password or tick “Disable password protection”.

cookie.title: Stay logged in
cookie.desc: How long you stay logged in to the web interface after signing in. Browsers usually cap cookies at ~400 days.

clean.title: Clean SD card
clean.prompt: Removes macOS metadata from the SD card (.DS_Store, ._* files, Spotlight and trash folders). Music files are left untouched. Continue?

directory.title: Directory
directory.desc: Upload directory with all files and subdirectories

upload.title: Upload
upload.selectFile: Please select files or folders to upload!

fileurl.title: File, directory or URL (^ and # aren't allowed as chars)

mode.12: Single track of a directory (random). Followed by sleep.
mode.15: All tracks of a directory + subdirectories (sorted, recursive)
mode.17: All tracks of a directory + subdirectories (random, recursive)
mode.7: All tracks of a directory (sorted, loop)
mode.9: All tracks of a directory (random, loop)
mode.13: All tracks of a random subdirectory (sorted)
mode.14: All tracks of a random subdirectory (random)
mode.11: List (files from SD and/or webstreams) from local .m3u-File

cmd.107: Sleep after end of five tracks

learn.waiting: Now place the RFID card on the reader…
learn.virtualLabel: Or assign a virtual card (e.g. to trigger via MQTT/command only):

mqtt.title: MQTT-settings
mqtt.restartNote: Note: MQTT settings only take effect after restarting the device.
mqtt.restartPrompt: MQTT settings were saved. Restart now to apply changes?

ftp.title: FTP-settings
ftp.authHint: FTP accepts any username - only the password matters. The password is the shared device password ("Security" tab). An empty password means access without a login.

pwd.title: FTP-password
pwd.securityHint: The FTP password is set on the "Security" tab (shared device password).

start.title: Start FTP-server
start.desc: Enables FTP-server until device is restarted.

webdav.title: WebDAV settings
webdav.desc: Mounts the SD card as a network drive so you can copy audio files onto it straight from your PC/Mac (connect to it as a WebDAV server in Finder/Explorer).

auth.hint: WebDAV accepts any username - only the password matters. The password is the shared device password ("Security" tab). An empty password means access without a login.

start.title: Start WebDAV server
start.desc: Enables the WebDAV server until the device is restarted.

sync.title: File synchronization
sync.intro: Downloads files from a web server (e.g. nginx) onto the SD card. The ESPuino fetches a JSON manifest from the URL below and downloads every file listed in it that is missing locally or has a different size. By default it is additive – local files are kept; the option further down can also delete local files the manifest no longer lists (mirror mode). Syncing only runs while nothing is being played from the SD card.
sync.pwd.hint: Username and password are sent to the server via HTTP Basic Auth (e.g. nginx auth_basic).
sync.abortBtn.hint: A running sync is aborted as soon as a button is pressed (or a command is triggered).
sync.delete: Delete local files that are not in the manifest
sync.deleteHint: Caution – mirror mode: after downloading, local files missing from the manifest are deleted. System and config files (hidden files, manifest.json, stats.csv, backup.txt) are kept. Only runs when the manifest loaded completely.

run.title: Sync now
run.desc: Fetches the manifest and downloads missing/changed files. May take a while depending on the amount of data.
run.gotools: Open sync in the Tools tab
run.dryHint: Dry run: shows what the sync would download and (with mirror mode on) delete — without changing anything.
run.dryDone: Dry run complete — see the report below

manifest.title: Manifest format
manifest.desc: The manifest is a JSON file listing all files to sync. Place it on the web server (the URL above points to it):
manifest.fields: path: path relative to the manifest's directory – used both as the download URL (manifest directory + path) and as the target path on the SD card. size: file size in bytes – used to detect whether the local file is up to date.
manifest.nginx: nginx example: put the audio files and manifest.json into a directory served via a location block; for a login add auth_basic + auth_basic_user_file.

rfid.title: RFID card sync
rfid.desc: Syncs RFID card assignments with a central server (manifest.php) and/or other ESPuinos (P2P). On conflict the most recently changed assignment wins.
rfid.peersHint: On learn the card is pushed to each peer. Each peer has an IP/hostname + web password.
rfid.runDesc: Pulls all cards from the server, merges them with the local ones (newest wins) and pushes them to the server and peers.
rfid.phpHint: Server script (dynamic, generates the manifest + RFID sync):
rfid.peerKeyPlaceholder: web password of the other ESPuinos

backup.title: Auto-backup to server
backup.desc: Uploads a full configuration backup (settings, RFID assignments, EQ rules and listening statistics) to a server – the same backup the export button produces, so it can be restored via import. Passwords are never included. Uses the username/password from the file-sync section above.
backup.auto: Back up automatically once a day
backup.autoHint: Once a day (after the clock is set and WiFi is up) the backup is uploaded to the server in the background.

playlist.title: Create playlist
playlist.addFolder: Add all tracks in this folder
playlist.name: File name

sink.title: ESPuino as Bluetooth speaker
sink.desc: ESPuino is started as a Bluetooth speaker.

source.title: Connect with Bluetooth headphone
source.desc: The device connects to the specified Bluetooth headset.

scan.title: Scan for devices
scan.notAllowed: Bluetooth device scan not allowed in current mode. Please switch to Bluetooth headphone mode first.

switchmode.title: Bluetooth Mode Active
switchmode.prompt: Control via website is restricted in Bluetooth mode. Would you like to switch back to WiFi mode and restart?

equalizer.title: Equalizer
equalizer.info: According to the library (ESP32-audioI2S), the frequencies are 500 Hz LowShelf, 3000 Hz PeakEQ and 6000 Hz HighShelf. The "Audiobook / Speech" and "Deep voices" profiles cut the bass and lift mids/highs so deep narrator voices stay intelligible.

rfid.title: RFID Reader
rfid.description: Select the type of RFID reader connected to your ESPuino. Auto-detect will try to determine the connected reader type automatically.
rfid.pn5180LpcdExp: Enables Low Power Card Detection for PN5180 RFID reader. Only possible if solder jumpers are set correctly.

slix2Password.title: SLIX2 Password
slix2Password.description: 8-digit password in hexadecimal format (e.g. 01020304) for reading SLIX2 tags.

rfid.mfrc522GainExp: Sets the sensitivity of the MFRC522 RFID reader. Possible values are 0 (lowest sensitivity) to 7 (highest sensitivity). Default is 7.

options.title: Options
options.playMonoExp: If two speakers are installed, stereo can be played. More information here.
options.savePlayPosShutdownExp: When the box is switched off, the playback position for audio books is saved. More information here under SAVE_PLAYPOS_BEFORE_SHUTDOWN.
options.savePlayPosRfidChange: Remember playback position at RFID tag change
options.savePlayPosRfidChangeExp: When switching to a new RFID tag, the playback position is saved for audio books. Does not affect modification tags. More information here under SAVE_PLAYPOS_WHEN_RFID_CHANGE.
options.savePlayPosPeriodicExp: While an audio book plays, the position is saved to flash every ~30 s. This way an unexpected power loss (dead battery, hard power-off) only loses the last few seconds instead of falling back to the start of the track. Fork feature.
options.restartFreshDaysExp: If an audiobook has not been played for more than the given number of days, it starts over from the beginning the next time the card is placed, instead of resuming the saved position. Handy for kids who, after a break of a day or two, no longer remember where they were. 0 = off (always resume). Default: 1. Requires a valid clock (NTP/RTC). Fork feature.
options.minResumeSecExp: If the card is removed within the first few seconds of an audiobook, the resume point is reset to the very beginning instead of saving the couple of seconds played. So a card that was only briefly sampled starts cleanly from the start next time rather than a few seconds in. Only affects the very first track of position-saving audiobook modes, so real mid-book progress is never discarded. 0 = off. Default: 20. Fork feature.
options.seekStepExp: Jump distance in seconds for the "smart forward/backward" commands (CMD_SMART_FORWARDS/BACKWARDS). On an audiobook that is a single long file these buttons seek within the file; on a playlist of several files they change tracks instead. Several quick presses are coalesced into one jump (a single re-buffer instead of many). Default: 300 (5 min). Fork feature.
options.pauseIfRfidRemoved: Pause when RFID tag is removed
options.pauseIfRfidRemovedExp: Playback is paused when the RFID tag is removed. More information here under PAUSE_IF_RFID_REMOVED.
options.stopIfRfidRemoved: Stop instead of pause on removal
options.stopIfRfidRemovedExp: When enabled (only together with “Pause when RFID tag is removed”): removing the tag fully stops playback instead of just pausing, so the LEDs return to the idle animation. For audiobooks the exact position is saved the moment the tag is removed, so re-applying it continues right where you left off; other content starts over.
options.dontAcceptRfidTwice: Don't accept the same RFID twice
options.dontAcceptRfidTwiceExp: RFID tags are not accepted twice in a row. Cannot be used together with Pause when RFID tag is removed.
options.playLastRfidOnReboot: Play last RFID tag on restart
options.playLastRfidOnRebootExp: Play last RFID tag on restart. More information here under PLAY_LAST_RFID_ON_REBOOT.
options.pauseOnMinVolumeExp: Playback is paused when the volume reaches the minimum level. More information here
options.recoverVolBootExp: After a restart, the speaker remembers which volume was last used and automatically adjusts it again.
options.volumeCurveExp: Use of logarithmic volume calculation. Can help if the gradations in the lower volume range are too coarse.
options.playStartupSoundExp: Play the ready sound (the MP3 below) once on cold start. Disable for a silent boot.
options.noSleepWhenPoweredExp: When enabled, the device will not go into standby due to inactivity while it is on external power. Since there is no charge/USB sense, being 'on power' is estimated from the battery voltage (charge voltage sits above the resting voltage) — see threshold. The manual sleep timer is unaffected.
options.noSleepWhenPoweredWarn: Note: if the device stays powered and never sleeps, the scarce internal heap fragments over many hours and the web interface gets progressively slower to load. Remedy: reboot occasionally, or leave this option off so standby (= a cold boot on wake-up) defragments the heap.
options.poweredVoltageExp: At or above this battery voltage the device is considered 'on power'. For LiFePO4, ~3.5 V is a good starting point (charge voltage ~3.6 V, full resting ~3.3-3.4 V).

battery.title: Battery
battery.lowWarning: Show warning below this threshold (in volt)
battery.lowCritical: Lowest voltage (in volt), that is indicated by one LED
battery.criticalShutoff: Below this voltage (in volt), ESPuino turns off
battery.high: Voltage (in volt), that is indicated by all LEDs

playlist.title: Playlist
playlist.sortMode: Sorting mode for playlist and file browser
playlist.recDepth: Maximum recursion depth at generation of recursive playlists

branding.title: Branding
branding.hint: Leave empty for the default. Replaces "ESPuino" in the header and footer.

eqrules.title: EQ rules (per file/folder)
eqrules.desc: Overview of the per-path equalizer profiles. View and delete them here.
eqrules.full: EQ rule storage is full — delete some existing rules before adding new ones.
eqrules.savefailed: Could not save the EQ rule.

backup.title: Backup
backup.desc: Exports or imports all settings and RFID assignments as a JSON file.

import.title: Import
import.desc: Select and import a backup file. Some settings (e.g. MQTT) require a restart afterwards.

nvs.title: Saved RFID assignments
nvs.desc: The RFID assignments are not stored on the SD card but on the internal, non-volative storage (NVS) of the ESPuino.

list.title: List of all stored RFID assignments

erase.title: Erase all RFID assignments
erase.desc:
Via importer new entries will only be inserted but old ones won't be erased. Only in case an old assignment to the same entry already exists, it will be overwritten. With this function all existing RFID assignments will be erased.
Further information (in German).

import.title: Import
import.desc: A backup file can be uploaded here in order to import RFID assignments.

export.title: Export
export.desc: A backup file can be downloaded here in order to export the RFID assignments.

format.title: Format SD card
format.desc: Creates a fresh FAT filesystem on the SD card. This permanently erases everything — all music, playlists and covers. The card stays mounted afterwards.
format.prompt: Really format the SD card? All files (music, playlists, covers) will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.

fwupdate.title: Firmware-Update
fwupdate.desc: Firmware can be updated right here.

github.desc: The device downloads the latest firmware straight from GitHub (HTTPS) and installs it itself.
github.confirm: Download and install the latest firmware from GitHub? The device will restart afterwards.
github.uptodate: Already up to date – no update needed.
github.started: Device is installing the firmware and will restart – the page will reload shortly…

forum.title: Forum
forum.desc:
Having problems or aim to discuss about ESPuino?
Join us at ESPuino-Forum! Especially there's a lot of (german) documentation online!

swagger.title: Swagger
swagger.desc: Documentation of the REST API: Swagger.

systeminfo.title: Information
systeminfo.sdcard: SD card: {{free}} GB free of {{total}} GB
systeminfo.hallsensor: HallEffectSensor NullFieldValue: {{hsnullfieldvalue}}, actual: {{hsactual}}, diff:{{hsdiff}}, LastWaitFor_State:{{hslastwaitforstate}} (waited:{{hswaited}} ms)

homekit.title: Apple HomeKit
homekit.intro: Add this ESPuino to the Apple Home app to control playback, volume and the button-lock with Siri and automations. Pairs over your existing WiFi — no extra hardware needed.
homekit.enablehint: When disabled, HomeSpan does not start at boot — this saves boot time if you don't use HomeKit.
homekit.enablereboot: Setting saved. Restart the ESPuino now to apply it?
homekit.howto: In the Home app tap + → Add Accessory → More options… → ESPuino, then scan the QR code (or enter the code manually).
homekit.namefollowshint: The device and remote names follow the device name from the General settings automatically. After you rename the device, the new name only applies to new pairings — the Home app keeps the name you assigned when pairing. To update it, rename the accessory inside the Home app, or reset the pairing here and add the ESPuino again.
homekit.restartnote: Takes effect after a restart of the ESPuino.
homekit.actionsnote: “Regenerate code” creates a new pairing code + QR (the old one stops working; already-paired homes keep working). “Reset pairing” removes all paired controllers so the player can be added to a different Home.
homekit.confirmreset: Remove all HomeKit pairings from this ESPuino?
homekit.confirmregen: Generate a new pairing code? The current code and QR will stop working for new pairings.
homekit.namessaved: Names saved. Restart the ESPuino now to apply them?
